beats.title: Debating the Mountain Route
beats.boundaryReason: Opening discussion scene at the mountain pass ends when the party starts actively searching for the hidden route.
dateEvidence: Session manifest date is 1748-04-12.
DM opening establishes mid-morning at the mountain pass.
This beat is continuous planning before the search begins.

beats.title: Rune Stones and Gorge
beats.boundaryReason: A short investigative scene covers locating the numbered guide stones, identifying the gorge entrance, and testing the stream water.
dateEvidence: Same-day continuation from the morning approach.
Session note timeline places discovery of the back door around noon.
No rest or overnight transition occurs.

beats.title: Flooded Tunnel Entrance
beats.boundaryReason: The party leaves the horses behind, pushes through the flooded entry tunnel, and drains the water barrier by pulling the hidden chain.
dateEvidence: Continuous movement from the gorge into the tunnel.
Still part of the noon discovery and entry sequence.
No date change or long pause is indicated.

beats.title: Escape Hatch and False Rubble
beats.boundaryReason: After clearing the water trap, the party explores the guard passage, opens the escape hatch, passes the illusionary rubble, and finds the hidden wall mechanism.
dateEvidence: Continuous exploration immediately after draining the tunnel.
Still before the encounter with the cavern inhabitants.
No date transition is suggested.

beats.title: Inakara's Bargain
beats.boundaryReason: The hidden door opens into the inhabited cavern, leading to first contact with Inakara and her people and ending when she shoves the party toward the promised fight.
dateEvidence: Session note timeline marks the meeting with Inakara in the early afternoon.
This is a direct continuation after entering the cavern complex.
No rest or date change is present.

beats.title: The Roper Strikes
beats.boundaryReason: Combat begins as the party enters the trading hall, the roper ambushes Wellby, and the group starts figuring out how its tentacles and stone hide work.
dateEvidence: Immediate continuation from Inakara leading the party to the threat.
Session note timeline places the roper fight in the early afternoon.
Same-day combat with no intervening break.

beats.title: Learning the Safe Distance
beats.boundaryReason: The middle of the fight focuses on repeated grapples, repositioning, and the dawning realization that the roper can barely move beyond its tentacle reach.
dateEvidence: Mid-combat continuation with no scene break.
The fight remains in the same cavern and same stretch of the afternoon.
No date or time jump occurs.

beats.title: Wearing the Roper Down
beats.boundaryReason: Once the party establishes the safe radius, the combat resolves as they kite the roper to death and secure Inakara's promised passage before ending the session.
dateEvidence: Final phase of the same afternoon combat.
The session ends immediately after the roper dies and passage is discussed.
No date change or rest intervenes.
